title: Double-path (LAPACK) backend foundation: build wiring + zheev/zgesvd candidates + restore double-vs-arb@53 cross-check
description: LAPACK now available (aic-5ty closed). Add the fast/double path as an audition candidate: (1) wire LAPACKE into the build (LIBS += -llapacke -llapack -lblas; lapacke.h); (2) acb_mat <-> column-major complex-double conversion helpers; (3) double-path candidates for the heavy ops using LAPACKE_zheev (Hermitian eig - NOTE: handles DEGENERATE spectra, unlike FLINT's certified simple-eig aic-w4o.1, so it is the fast route for projection/multiplicity inputs) and LAPACKE_zgesvd/zgesdd (operator norm / singular values / full SVD aic-w4o.2); optionally double Newton-Schulz sgn; (4) restore the double-vs-arb@53 cross-check in tests/aic_test.h + retrofit mat/funcalc auditions to include the LAPACK candidate with Pareto comparison (Law 4). Double path is FAST/uncertified; arb remains the certified path.

title: Implement adversarial instance generators (acb_mat evil-matrix corpus)
description: Implement the families from docs/adversarial/{nla,domain}.md as C generators over acb_mat, each with a tunable 'evil knob' (t, eps, delta, eta, gap, kappa, dim, multiplicity) for mild->lethal sweeps, with FIXED seeds for reproducibility. Provides the instances consumed by all other tasks in this epic and by the shared corpus aic-w4o.5 (build them together / unify). Prioritize the merged lethal shortlist in docs/adversarial/README.md.

title: Dimension-sweep regression: certified constants must NOT grow with dim (universality canary)
description: THE highest-value correctness test surfaced by the research: sweep dim A and assert the universal constants (c_0 from cor_improvement; the O(eps)/O(eta) constants in th_main / factorization) stay BOUNDED, independent of dim. Catches a constant ~ dim A (e.g. accidental Haar-measure diagonal instead of the explicit B-diagonal, tex:1249-1254 vs tex:484) that is invisible on any single instance. Directly tests the paper's universality claim. Blocked by the modules it tests (cstar_build, errreduce).

title: Adversarial / correctness stress suite (punishing benchmark + test instances)
description: VITAL FOR CORRECTNESS (user directive 2026-05-28). The happy path is the least useful. Every module's tests and benchmarks must be driven by punishing, adversarial instances that push every edge case: rank-deficient / near-singular, degenerate & clustered spectra (gaps ~eps), highly non-normal (sensitive eigenvectors, large pseudospectra; cf. the tex:540 t^1/3 example), ill-conditioned / wide dynamic range, and near-boundary precondition inputs (||X^2-I||->1, delta->1/4, eta->1/4). Correct behavior on an evil input = the certified bound provably holds OR the routine fails loud (no plausible-but-wrong output). This epic is the track; it feeds the shared benchmark corpus (aic-w4o.5). See memory feedback-adversarial-benchmarks.

title: Deep-research catalog of the most adversarial instances (evil-matrix corpus)
description: Produce a structured catalog mapping each adversarial instance family -> why it is evil -> which AIC operations it attacks (sgn/abs/theta, eig, opnorm, inverse, polar, contraction, Choi/Kraus, projection, compressed product) -> a concrete generator recipe (acb_mat) -> expected correct behavior (certified bound holds, or fail-loud refusal). Cover: classic NLA torture matrices (Jordan/defective, Kahan, Frank, Grcar, Wilkinson, Hilbert, companion, scaled/graded), non-normality & pseudospectra (Davies, the tex:540 t^1/3 block), rank-deficient/near-singular, degenerate/clustered spectra with gaps ~eps, ill-conditioning & catastrophic cancellation in (XY)Z-X(YZ) and Phi^2-Phi, near-boundary precondition inputs, and DOMAIN-SPECIFIC evil: near-singular/low-rank Choi matrices, extremal channels, cb-vs-op ampliation-sensitive idempotence defects, near-trivial projections P~+-I, the eps~c/n constant-blowup regime (tex:484). Output written to docs/adversarial/ as markdown. This research informs the implementation sub-tasks under this epic.
close_reason: Catalogs written: docs/adversarial/nla.md (8 NLA families) + domain.md (6 domain families + sweep matrix + oracle), synthesized in README.md (merged lethal shortlist, cross-cutting findings). Implementation tasks filed: aic-dbo.2 (generators), aic-dbo.3 (dim-sweep universality canary), aic-dbo.4 (per-module retrofit), aic-dbo.5 (boundary/fail-loud).

title: Certified eig for DEGENERATE Hermitian spectra (projections, C* multiplicities)
description: T-mat found FLINT 3.0.1 has NO Hermitian eigensolver and its certified path acb_mat_eig_simple REQUIRES a simple (non-degenerate) spectrum (returns 0 -> we abort on repeated eigenvalues). But this project is dominated by degenerate spectra: projections have eigenvalues 0/1 with high multiplicity; the target algebras are direct sums of matrix blocks (B(L_j)) with multiplicities. So certified eigendecomposition of degenerate Hermitian matrices is foundational and currently missing. Audition (Law 4) certified routes: acb_mat_eig_multiple / acb_mat_eig_multiple_rump (cluster/invariant-subspace enclosures); spectral-gap clustering then per-cluster enclosure; or projector-based deflation. Must handle exact 0/1 multiplicities. Cross-check vs simple-spectrum path on non-degenerate inputs; eta=0 / exact-projection oracle.

title: Double-path LA backend: LAPACK dev unavailable — backend is an audition
description:
Env has FLINT 3.0.1 (arb/acb bundled) and links+runs fine. But LAPACK is runtime-only: no liblapack.so/libblas.so dev symlink, no lapacke.h/cblas.h, '-llapack' fails (cannot find -llapack). Passwordless sudo unavailable, so cannot apt-install. User can unblock the LAPACK route with: sudo apt-get install -y liblapack-dev liblapacke-dev libblas-dev.

DECISION (Law 4 audition; Law 2 reuse-when-available): the double/fast path's linear-algebra backend is itself an auditioned choice. Until LAPACK dev is present, the FLINT/arb path is the foundation and the prec=53 acb path is the fast anchor. The cross-check ladder temporarily becomes 'acb@53 vs acb@high-prec' instead of 'double(LAPACK) vs arb@53'. Candidates to audition for the fast path when unblocked: (a) LAPACK (zheev/zgesvd/zgees) via extern Fortran prototypes or LAPACKE; (b) FLINT acb_mat at low prec (acb_mat_approx_eig_qr etc.); (c) Eigen3 (present, but C++). Do not presume a winner.
close_reason: Resolved: user installed liblapack-dev/liblapacke-dev/libblas-dev (2026-05-28). LAPACKE links+runs (lapacke.h present; cblas.h not needed). Double/fast path is now an active audition candidate; cross-check ladder restores double(LAPACK)-vs-arb@53. Implementation tracked in the new double-path foundation bead.

title: Audition: functional calculus sgn(X), |X|, theta(X), x^alpha
description: Realizes fc-abs-sgn (tex 514-522) and prop_P (tex 524). Candidate routes to audition (NOT decisions): Denman-Beavers iteration vs Newton-Schulz vs Hermitian eigendecomposition vs Taylor. Precondition ||X^2-I||<1 asserted. Arb-critical: |X| near-singular, spectral fragility (tex 540). Shard B.
close_reason: funcalc delivered: sgn (Newton-Schulz default + Denman-Beavers, eig-free), abs, theta, xpow (Taylor, tex:503-511), prop_P (tex:524-533, delta<1/4 guard). Audition: NS faster at all sizes AND both handle degenerate projectors to 1e-40 (eig route non-viable per aic-w4o.1, recorded). 10 cross-checks incl. degenerate-input + candidate-agreement, mutation-proven; benchmarks emitted.

title: Audition harness: red-green TDD + correctness + performance benchmarks
description: The core exploratory engine. For any routine, support: (a) write failing assertion first; (b) correctness gates = double-vs-arb@prec=53 agreement, exact special cases, and the global eta=0 oracle (exact idempotent => exact Choi-Effros C* structure, zero defect); (c) performance benchmarking so algorithm candidates are ranked on speed too. NO algorithm is presumed fit; the harness exists to audition competing candidates and retire losers. Mirrors su2-fft make test (~5s) + PROFILING.md discipline.
close_reason: Harness built: tests/aic_test.h (fail-loud asserts + acb/acb-mat agreement checkers for the temp acb@53-vs-high ladder), bench/aic_bench.h (ns/op timer), bench_smoke + test_harness (9 checks, mutation-proven). make test+bench pass, zero warnings. eta=0 oracle + double-vs-LAPACK gate documented as deferred.

title: Milestone: eta=0 vertical slice (mat->funcalc->ucp->idemp_structure)
description: First integration target (MODULE_PLAN.md 'suggested first vertical slice'): drive an EXACTLY idempotent Phi through mat->funcalc->ucp->idemp_structure; validate via the eta=0 oracle (reconstruct M,A,Delta,Gamma exactly; rebuild Phi and compare; zero defect). Fast regression base before the harder projection/cstar_build work.

title: Audition: matrix substrate (norms, Herm eig, SVD, Kronecker, partial trace)
description: Complex matrix type, double (LAPACK zheev/zgesvd/zgees) + arb (acb_mat). Operations needed downstream: operator norm (via SVD), Frobenius norm, Hermitian eigensolve, SVD, Kronecker tensor product, partial trace, reshape. Audition implementations on correctness+perf. Layer 0.
close_reason: mat substrate delivered: Frobenius/operator norm, Hermitian eig (values+vectors, certified-enclosure simple-spectrum path), singular values, Kronecker, partial trace (left-major convention pinned, matches tex:263-288). 14 cross-checks pass (eig reconstruction, kron/ptrace identities, prec 53-vs-256 ladder), mutation-proven; benchmarks emitted. Limitations tracked: aic-w4o.1 (degenerate eig, P1), aic-w4o.2 (full SVD), aic-w4o.3 (opnorm audition), aic-w4o.4 (test split).

title: Scaffold dual number-path build (C11 + FLINT/arb + LAPACK)
description: include/aic.h API + storage-layout contract; Makefile (make test, make bench); link FLINT>=3.0 (arb/acb) + LAPACK/BLAS; flags -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wshadow -Wstrict-prototypes -O2 -g -std=c11. Skeleton for double path and acb path side by side. No algorithms yet.
close_reason: Scaffold complete: include/aic.h, src/aic.c, tests/test_smoke.c, Makefile (-lflint only), .gitignore. make test passes, zero warnings, FLINT/arb link verified. LAPACK deferred per aic-5ty.

title: Audition: UCP map representations (Choi/Stinespring/Kraus) + conversions + cb-norm + carrier
description: Core data structures (shard G): Choi matrix C_Phi, isometry V (Phi(X)=V^dag(X tensor 1_F)V), Kraus K_a. Conversions; CP<=>Choi PSD; unital<=>partial trace=I; cb-norm (via Choi/SDP); carrier (lem_carrier tex 1724, cor_carrier 1731). Audition representations + cb-norm estimators on correctness+perf. Layer 1.

title: Infrastructure: dual-path build + TDD/benchmark harness
description: Project scaffolding for the C+FLINT/arb cores and the audition harness. Everything downstream is exploratory: no algorithm is presumed fit; each is red-green TDD auditioned against correctness AND performance benchmarks. See CLAUDE.md, MODULE_PLAN.md.

title: Foundations: matrix substrate, functional calculus, UCP reps
description: Layer 0-1 of the module DAG (MODULE_PLAN.md): mat, funcalc, contraction, ucp. Substrate everything else builds on. Shards B (analytic tools) and G (UCP maps).

title: Audition: idempotent-structure algorithm (th_idemp_structure)
description: Exact idempotent UCP Phi -> subspace M, C* algebra A, UCP maps Delta, Gamma with Gamma C_M Delta=1_A, Delta Gamma C_M=Phi (tex 318 stmt / 2055 proof). Steps: carrier M, A=Img(Phi) eigenspace-1, Delta inclusion, Gamma (prop_Gamma 2106), homomorphism check (lem_idemp 1916). The cleanest theorem for the eta=0 oracle. Audition carrier/eigenspace extraction on correctness+perf. Shard G.

title: Extend bench harness: multi-dimensional perf metrics + Pareto-domination reporting
description: Per the Pareto-audition rule (CLAUDE.md Law 4, memory feedback-pareto-audition): the harness must emit several perf columns per candidate so Pareto analysis is possible, not just one ns/op. Add: wall time (have), iteration count, peak memory, accuracy / certified-ball tightness at fixed prec, and robustness on adversarial inputs (ties to epic aic-dbo). Provide a small Pareto-frontier reporter that flags domination / non-domination across candidates and regimes. Motivating case: the contraction Picard-vs-Anderson tie (aic-n3n).

title: Near-boundary / fail-loud guard stress tests (precondition edges)
description: Push each precondition right to the edge and just over it: ||X^2-I|| -> 1^- and the sub-ULP straddle of =1 (nla 7a); prop_P delta -> 1/4^- (nla 7b); contraction c -> 1^- (nla 7c); eta -> 1/4^- (domain 2A). Assert the arb guard ABORTS (fail-loud) when the certified ball straddles the boundary -- exactly the case where double arithmetic is unconditionally wrong -- and proceeds with a certified result strictly inside. The sgn domain guard (the fragility chokepoint per the research) gets the hardest stress.

title: Policy+retrofit: every module's tests/benches draw adversarial instances (bound-holds OR fail-loud)
description: Make it a standing rule and retrofit existing modules (mat, funcalc, contraction, ...): each module's test/bench must exercise the relevant adversarial families and assert one of two acceptable outcomes on each evil input -- the certified bound provably holds, OR the routine fails loud (assert/refuse). 'Passes on nice inputs' counts as not yet tested.

title: Shared, parameterized benchmark-instance corpus (representative regimes)
description: Current benchmarks use ad-hoc per-module instances (small n<=16, deterministic, single-instance; mat eig even uses a deliberately well-SEPARATED spectrum = the easy case, not the project's real degenerate projections). For Law-4 performance auditioning to be meaningful and comparable across modules, build a shared instance generator (e.g. include/aic_instances.h or bench helpers) parameterized by the regimes that actually matter in arXiv:2405.02434: (a) eta-idempotent UCP maps at varying eta and dim (Choi/Kraus); (b) eps-C* algebras / near-projections at varying eps; (c) DEGENERATE vs non-degenerate Hermitian spectra and block-multiplicity structure (direct sums of B(L_j)); (d) a dim sweep (n up to where arb certified ops stay tractable) with FIXED random seeds for reproducibility. Retrofit existing benches (mat/funcalc) to draw from it. Make perf numbers comparable and tied to real workloads, not toy inputs.

title: Full SVD (U, Sigma, V) for acb matrices
description: T-mat produced singular VALUES only (via eig of A^dag A). Full SVD (U,Sigma,V) has no FLINT routine; audition Golub-Kahan bidiagonalisation + implicit QR vs one-sided Jacobi, with certified enclosures. Candidate dependency for T-unitary polar decomposition and T-ucp Stinespring; not a hard block (polar can go via Hermitian eig of A^dag A).

title: Audition: incremental C* construction = master loop (proof of th_main)
description: The central algorithm (shard E master loop): B as partitioned index set + matrix units E_jk; Stage1 commutative skeleton, Stage2 extend blocks (lem_extension tex 1378), Stage3 merge (cor_merge_sum 1352, lem_add_dim 1363, lem_merging 1325); cor_improvement after each step keeps delta_0=c_0*eps. Output O(eps)-iso v:B->A. Audition orderings/heuristics on correctness+perf; assert constant does NOT grow with dim.

title: Research: extract explicit universal constant c_0 (cor_improvement) + verify dim-independence
description: cor_improvement's constant fixes the O(eps) constant in th_main. Extract it explicitly and confirm (empirically + by argument) it does NOT grow with dim A (the failure mode of the naive Haar/cohomology route, tex 484). MODULE_PLAN.md escalation 3.

title: Research: reconstruct th_factorization proof closure (CP-ization steps 4-5)
description: th_factorization's proof is an OUTLINE; the UCP CP-ization (unitary 1-design) and decode Upsilon (lem_RC) constructions are prose, not a closed proof linking to the stated bounds DelUps/UpsDel2 (shard H). Reconstruct the closure before T-factorize is trustworthy. MODULE_PLAN.md escalation 4.

title: Research: finite N for cb-norm truncation (conjecture n <= dim A)
description: cb-norm is a sup over all ampliations 1_{M_n} tensor (.). Numerically 'for all n' must be truncated. Determine/prove the finite N (conjecture n <= dim A, shard F) needed before T-opspace can claim a certified cb-bound. MODULE_PLAN.md escalation 2.

title: Research: prove an Omega(1) spectral gap always exists for dim A>1
description: T-projection Route A relies on a Hermitian element of A having a usable spectral gap. The paper proves existence of a nontrivial projection topologically; we need the structural finite-dim guarantee that a gap exists (or a certified failure mode). Until proven, projection auditions certify per-instance and escalate on failure. MODULE_PLAN.md escalation 1.

title: Open research questions / escalations
description: Constructivization gaps the paper leaves open (MODULE_PLAN.md escalations). Tracked separately so module work can proceed with per-instance certification while proofs are pursued.

title: Audition: associated eps-C* algebra of an eta-idempotent map (th_almost_idemp)
description: th_almost_idemp (tex 2192): Phi_tilde=theta(2Phi-1) regularization; A=Img(Phi_tilde); star-product => extended O(eta)-C* algebra. Key: associativity eqs Phi_assoc1/Phi_assoc2 (proofs at 2208/2239/2639). Audition the regularization + structure verification on correctness+perf. Shard H.

title: Audition: delta-homomorphism approximation (lem_approx, prop_delta_hominc)
description: lem_approx (tex 1224-1316): approximate a delta-homomorphism by a genuine homomorphism via the multiplicativity defect (Newton-type iteration). prop_delta_hominc (1194). Audition iteration variants on correctness+perf. Shard E.

title: Main theorem construction: dhom, errreduce, cstar_build, opspace
description: Layer 4-6 (MODULE_PLAN.md): delta-homomorphism approximation, error reduction, the incremental C* construction (the master loop = proof of th_main), and operator-space/cb-norm tensor extension (th_main_ext). Shards E and F.

title: Audition: compression maps & corner algebra (lem_alpha/PQ_Hilb/PQR/1d_proj)
description: Co_{P,Q} compressions, corner subspaces S_{P,Q}, compressed product, block bijection alpha (lem_alpha tex 1086), Hilbert structure on 1-dim corners (lem_PQ_Hilb 1123), lem_PQR (1162), lem_1d_proj (1179), equivalence classes. Mostly explicit/constructive. Shard D.

title: Audition: eps-C* algebra data model + axiom-defect estimators
description: Represent A as operator subspace of M_N with star-product (Choi-Effros X*Y=Phi(XY)), norm, involution, unit. Estimators for associativity defect (tex 413), C*-defect (428), unit defect (432). Definitions tex 407/443. Audition norm/defect estimators on correctness+perf. Shards A,B.

title: Audition: constructive nontrivial-projection finder (CONSTRUCTIVIZATION CRUX)
description: lem_nontriv_projection (tex 931) is proved NON-CONSTRUCTIVELY (Lefschetz-Hopf + H-space). Finite-dim candidate routes to audition (shard D, none presumed): Route A Hermitian eigensolve + spectral-gap split + theta(2P-I) snap + arb gap certification; Route B near-involution on U; Route C minimize ||P^2-P|| over Hermitian P bounded from +-I. Audition all on correctness+perf; keep multiple alive until benchmarks retire one. Arb-critical: gap ~ eps.

title: Audition: Banach fixed-point inverse/implicit-function solver (lem_invfun)
description: lem_invfun (tex 564-592) IS a constructive algorithm: the iteration x_n = g_y(x_{n-1}), g_y(x)=x+V^{-1}(y-f(x)), with rate c<1. Audition stopping criteria / acceleration variants on correctness+perf. Shard B.
close_reason: contraction solver from lem_invfun (tex:564-592): Picard + Anderson(1), callback-based, fail-loud guards (c<1, ball-containment, Cauchy r*c^{n-1}). 30 cross-checks incl. lemma norm bounds, Cauchy rate, sgn-system vs aic_sgn to 1e-25; mutation-proven (2 RED). Audition PARETO outcome: neither dominates - Anderson dominates the slow/large-c regime (wall time + iters), near-tie in the fast/small-c regime; both kept on the frontier, regime-dispatched, default Picard (lem_invfun's targeted fast regime). Zero warnings.

title: Headline: idempotent structure & channel factorization
description: Layer 7 (MODULE_PLAN.md): th_idemp_structure algorithm, associated eps-C* algebra of an eta-idempotent map (th_almost_idemp), and the end-to-end factorization (th_factorization). Shards G and H.

title: Audition: approximate unitary group (polar decomp + group ops)
description: prop_polar (tex 809), lem_gV (758), lem_U_delta (699). Candidate polar-factor routes to audition: SVD vs Newton-Schulz; charts via contraction (lem_gV). Group ops mu, sigma. Substrate for projection. Shard C.

title: Audition: error reduction via explicit diagonal (cor_improvement)
description: cor_improvement (tex 1317): improve a delta-inclusion to a delta_0=O(eps)-inclusion, constant independent of delta and of dim. Constructive: explicit Heisenberg-Weyl diagonal of the GENUINE C* algebra B (no n-blowup, unlike naive Haar tex 484). Audition implementation + verify dimension-independence empirically. Shard E.

title: Audition: end-to-end channel factorization (th_factorization)
description: Headline 7-step pipeline (shard H): regularize Phi -> associated A -> th_main_ext gives B,v -> CP-ize Delta (unitary 1-design) -> decode Upsilon (lem_RC tex 2840) -> unitalize -> verify ||Delta Upsilon - Phi||_cb=O(eta). Dual: Enc=Upsilon*, Dec=Delta*. PROOF IS AN OUTLINE (see R-factclosure). Audition the UCP realizations on correctness+perf; certify composite O(eta) bound in arb.

title: Epsilon-algebra machinery: ecstar, unitary, projection, corner
description: Layer 2-3 (MODULE_PLAN.md): the eps-C* algebra data model, exact-unit fix, approximate unitary group, the constructive nontrivial-projection finder, and corner/compression algebra. Shards C (unitaries) and D (projection/subspaces).

title: Audition: operator-space / cb-norm ampliation + th_main_ext
description: def:opspace (tex 1453), prop_inc_ext (1483), lem_approx_ext (1508), th_main_ext (1538): control all ampliations 1_{M_n} tensor (.) so the iso is cb-bounded. Open: which finite N truncates 'for all n' (see R-cbtrunc). Audition the extended construction + N-truncation on correctness+perf. Shard F.

title: Split tests/test_mat.c (340 LOC) to honor ~200 LOC rule
description: tests/test_mat.c is 340 LOC, over the ~200 LOC house limit. Split into test_mat_norms.c / test_mat_eig.c / test_mat_struct.c. Pure refactor; behavior unchanged.
notes:
Also tests/test_funcalc.c is 288 LOC — split alongside test_mat.c.
Also tests/test_contraction.c is 323 LOC — split too.

title: Audition: operator-norm via power iteration on A^dag A
description: T-mat implemented operator norm via acb_mat_eig_global_enclosure on the Gram matrix (degeneracy-robust). Audition (Law 4) the cheaper power-iteration-on-A^dag-A route with a certified Rayleigh-quotient residual bound; compare correctness + performance against the current route.

title: Julia bindings: AlmostIdempotentChannels.jl (ccall)
description: Julia package wrapping the C cores via ccall (the su2-fft SU2FFT.jl pattern). High-level API + gold-standard cross-check in runtests.jl.

title: Julia ccall package AlmostIdempotentChannels.jl
description: ccall wrappers + accessors; local libaic.so build (deps/build.jl); gold-standard cross-check in test/runtests.jl. API: UCPMap (from Kraus/Choi), idemp_defect, associated_ecstar, main_isomorphism, factorize -> (B, Delta, Upsilon), channel_defect (certified O(eta) ball). Mirrors su2-fft SU2FFT.jl.

title: Audition: exact-unit normalization (prop_unit)
description: prop_unit (tex 672-688): achieve exact unit by O(eps)-change of unit and multiplication. Audition the normalization on correctness+perf. Shard B.
